likes:
What I like best about SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud) is how it combines simplicity, speed, and scalability in one modern platform.

A few highlights that really stand out:

Clean & intuitive Fiori UX – It’s much easier for end users to adopt, with role-based apps and real-time insights instead of heavy, complex screens.

Built-in best practices – Preconfigured business processes help companies go live faster and follow industry standards without reinventing the wheel.

Real-time analytics – Operational and analytical data in one system means instant reporting and smarter decision-making.

Automatic updates & innovation – No more worrying about upgrades; SAP continuously delivers new features, security, and compliance updates.

Scalability & flexibility – Easy to scale as the business grows, with strong integration capabilities across SAP BTP and third-party systems.

Overall, SAP Cloud ERP shifts the focus from system maintenance to business value and innovation, which is a big win for both IT teams and business users
dislikes:
If I’m being honest (and fair), SAP Cloud ERP is powerful—but it’s not without trade-offs. Here’s what I dislike most:

Limited customization (especially Public Cloud)
You can’t freely modify the system like in ECC or S/4HANA On-Prem. Many changes need to follow SAP’s standard processes, which can be frustrating for businesses with unique requirements.

Dependence on SAP’s release cycle
Mandatory quarterly/bi-annual updates mean you must constantly test changes. While innovation is great, it can impact stability if not managed well.

Higher total cost over time
Subscription fees, integration costs, and reliance on SAP BTP for extensions can make it expensive in the long run—especially for mid-size customers.

Complex integrations
Integrating with non-SAP or legacy systems often requires middleware (BTP/CPI), adding complexity and skill dependency.

Steep learning curve for consultants and users
Fiori, extensibility rules, and cloud restrictions require a mindset shift. Traditional SAP consultants often struggle initially.

Not ideal for highly localized or customized processes
Some country-specific or industry-specific needs still require workarounds or side-by-side solutions.

In short: SAP Cloud ERP is great for standardized, forward-looking organizations, but it can feel restrictive for companies that rely heavily on deep customization or legacy-heavy landscapes.
use_case:
SAP Cloud ERP (formerly SAP S/4HANA Cloud) is mainly solving the problem of complex, slow, and high-maintenance ERP landscapes, and that creates very real day-to-day benefits for me.

Here’s how I see it:

Eliminates heavy system maintenance
Traditional on-prem ERP requires constant upgrades, patching, and infrastructure management. With SAP Cloud ERP, this burden is removed, letting me focus more on process improvement and business outcomes rather than system upkeep.

Speeds up implementation and change
Preconfigured best practices and guided configuration reduce implementation time. For me, this means faster go-lives, quicker enhancements, and less firefighting during projects.

Solves real-time visibility issues
Earlier systems often depended on batch jobs and separate reporting tools. SAP Cloud ERP provides real-time analytics embedded in transactions, helping me make faster, data-driven decisions.

Reduces complexity across systems
By unifying operational and analytical data on a single HANA platform, it simplifies the landscape. This results in cleaner data models, fewer reconciliations, and more reliable reporting.

Supports scalability and future growth
As business requirements evolve, the cloud model scales easily. This benefits me by future-proofing solutions and reducing the risk of major re-implementation later.

Encourages process standardization
While sometimes restrictive, standardization actually helps streamline operations. For me, it leads to more predictable processes, better controls, and easier user adoption.

Overall, SAP Cloud ERP is solving the challenge of running an ERP that’s fast, modern, and innovation-ready, and it benefits me by saving time, reducing operational risk, and allowing me to focus on delivering real business value instead of maintaining the system. SAP Cloud ERP: Simple, Fast, Scalable—With Real-Time Insights and

likes: I really like the master data part of SAP Cloud ERP. Most of the master data reports can be fully customized using bookmarks on both filtering and reporting. It's also really handy that everything links to Excel, allowing direct export into spreadsheets without needing to mess around with Power Query or other CSV file converters. Additionally, using SAP Cloud ERP in a browser means I can have multiple tabs open and manage different tiles at the same time. The customized accesses are a big plus, letting me easily add and reach the tiles I need. Creating a customized welcome page with all the necessary tiles grouped as I wish is another feature I appreciate. Although setting up the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) can be challenging, once learned, it's perfectly adequate for tracking project performance. I also like that the code itself is customizable and it's relatively easy to link other applications to SAP, like time-reporting apps or delivery application add-ons. Overall, it's very good for detailed accounting, project management, and finance purposes, even though it requires a lot of time to set up properly and learn.
dislikes: I find the revenue recognition process frustrating because, unlike Oracle, time entry, costs, and revenue are not integrated into a single journal entry but are separate, making it hard to track revenue accurately. This separation results in difficulty pinpointing where revenue discrepancies come from. Another issue is having to manually add bill rates without the option to use a percentage multiplier, which I find cumbersome. Also, setting up SAP Cloud ERP is very difficult, which can be a real challenge initially and quite frustrating. Lastly, the user experience depends heavily on the setup being complete and error-free.

Switched from legacy on-premises ERP systems -SAP ECC because they were fragmented, required extensive manual effort, & lacked real-time visibility across finance, supply chain, & operations. 

The organisation needed a modern, cloud-based platform to improve efficiency, agility, and data-driven decision-making.

Compared to legacy systems, SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers real-time analytics, automated processes, & a unified platform that connects finance, procurement, project management, & operations. 

It simplifies integration, reduces operational overhead, & supports continuous innovation with quarterly updates making it far more agile, user-friendly, & future-proof.

likes: What I like most about SAP Cloud ERP is its real-time visibility, flexibility, and ease of integration. The system provides instant access to accurate data, which helps in faster decision-making and reduces manual work. I also appreciate its intuitive user interface, automated workflows, and robust process controls that support standardization across teams.
dislikes: Some standard processes and limited flexibility in navigation can be overwhelming and complex and not always easy to understand, requiring adjustments for frequent updates.
use_case:
SAP Cloud ERP simplifies previously disconnected workflows by providing SAP-managed integrated business processes, reducing the effort required to connect multiple systems.
This solves the problem of fragmented operations and manual integration.
I benefit: fewer system switches, consistent data, and smooth end-to-end processes. SAP Cloud ERP: Real-Time Visibility, Seamless Integration, and

dislikes: For some parts, the layout system is not good, and in the prepared GRN, some information is not perfectly designed. Also, the job work material issue system is a critical system, and it needs improvement.

likes: I like the fast speed of SAP Cloud ERP (formerly SAP S/4HANA Cloud), which is really helpful for my team. The initial setup was easy, especially with the integration between different modules like SS, MMPP, FI, and CO. It's been beneficial for configurations like the routing bill of material.

likes: I love SAP Cloud ERP's real-time data feature, which is very useful. The setup is great and I appreciate that it's accessible from anywhere since it's cloud-based. I also benefit from automated workflows that streamline processes easily.
dislikes: It may be difficult to comprehend since it's a data tool; not everyone can use it for sure. I mean it's a difficult area in general, perhaps making the interface a bit more intuitive. Not really easy to set up, it takes a while to get used to.
use_case: I manage finance, procurement, and reporting in one centralized system, eliminating manual processes and disconnected systems. This improves data accuracy and speeds up reporting. Simplifies Financial Processes, Needs Better

likes: I really like how fast everything runs—transactions and reports are super quick. For example, when I pull up inventory data, it refreshes in real-time, which helps a lot with decision-making. The cloud-based nature of it means I can access it from anywhere, whether I’m at the office or working remotely.
dislikes: sometimes integrating with older systems is a pain. For instance, we had issues syncing SAP with our CRM system. It took longer than expected, and we had to go back and forth with the IT team to troubleshoot connection problems.
use_case: Before, different departments had their own systems, which made it hard to get a clear picture of everything in one place. Now, with everything integrated, I can see the full picture in real-time—whether it’s financials, sales, or inventory—all in one system. This is saving me a lot of time and avoiding mistakes that would happen from manually reconciling data from different places.

likes: SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers impressive in-memory computing capabilities, providing real-time analytics and a streamlined data model. Its strong integration across finance, supply chain, and operations enhances overall efficiency. The Fiori interface makes the system more user-friendly, while features like automated upgrades, built-in analytics, and extensibility via SAP BTP contribute to a secure and future-ready ERP environment.
dislikes: One of the main challenges with SAP S/4HANA Cloud is its limited flexibility for deep customizations, especially when compared to traditional SAP on-premise systems. This can be restrictive for organizations with complex requirements. Adapting to SAP's standard best-practice processes often demands significant change management efforts. Users transitioning from legacy environments may also face a steep learning curve. To ensure a successful implementation, it is usually necessary to have experienced SAP professionals involved. Furthermore, both licensing and subscription costs can be relatively high, which is particularly impactful for small to mid-sized organizations, making a thorough cost-benefit analysis essential.
use_case:
Before using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, a lot of time was spent dealing with slow systems, disconnected data, and manual work just to get basic reports or answers. SAP S/4HANA Cloud brings everything into one integrated platform, so finance, supply chain, and operations are all working from the same real-time data. This has made it much easier to understand what’s actually happening in the business and respond quickly when something changes.

It has also reduced the day-to-day burden on IT and business teams. There’s no longer a constant focus on system maintenance or upgrades, and processes are more standardized and reliable. As a result, teams spend less time fixing issues or reconciling numbers and more time focusing on analysis, planning, and improving overall business performance. A Robust Cloud ERP with Strong Performance and

Its modern user interface and seamless integration across modules also make daily operations more efficient.
dislikes: What I dislike about SAP S/4HANA Cloud is that some configurations are limited compared to on-premise versions, which can make it challenging to customize certain processes. Additionally, system updates are frequent, and while they improve functionality, they sometimes require extra testing and adaptation on the business side.
use_case:

This has benefited us by improving process efficiency, enhancing visibility across departments, and enabling more accurate forecasting and planning. “A Powerful Cloud ERP With Room for

likes: I was introduced to SAP S/4HANA Cloud during my SAP Finance training. From my experience, it supports real-time financial reporting and helps make reconciliation tasks more organized within a cloud-based environment. In banking operations, this kind of system can assist with transaction monitoring, internal control work, and compliance-related processes such as KYC and AML checks. It also helps improve the accuracy of financial records and supports better audit follow-up.
dislikes: For users who are more familiar with traditional banking systems, it may take some time to understand all the available features and system functions. The learning curve at the beginning can be a bit challenging, especially for professionals transitioning from legacy financial platforms without prior ERP experience.
use_case: SAP Cloud ERP helps in organizing financial data and supports real-time reporting within a centralized platform. From a banking operations perspective, it can improve reconciliation processes, support compliance monitoring activities, and reduce manual errors in financial record management. This helps improve reporting accuracy and makes internal audit and financial control processes easier to manage. Steep Learning Curve for Legacy Users Despite Strong Real-Time

likes: SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a powerful and efficient ERP solution that helps organizations drive digital transformation. Its real-time data processing, intuitive user interface, and seamless integration with other SAP modules make it highly effective for business operations. The cloud-based model ensures flexibility, scalability, and faster deployment compared to on-premise solutions. It also provides strong analytics and reporting capabilities, enabling smarter decision-making. Overall, it is a reliable and future-ready platform that significantly enhances business efficiency and performance.
dislikes:
Some features like
Customization Limitations – Compared to on-premise, the cloud version offers less flexibility for heavy custom developments.

Cost Factor – Licensing and subscription costs can be high for small to mid-sized businesses.

Complex Implementation – Even with pre-configured best practices, implementation and migration can be complex and time-consuming.

Integration Challenges – Integrating with non-SAP third-party applications sometimes requires additional effort.

Learning Curve – Users and consultants may need extra training to adapt to the new system and Fiori interface.
use_case: SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a powerful and efficient ERP solution that helps organizations drive digital transformation. The cloud-based model ensures flexibility, scalability, and faster deployment compared to on-premise solutions. It also provides strong analytics and reporting capabilities, enabling smarter decision-making.

likes: SAP S/4HANA Cloud stands out for its real-time analytics, strong performance, and ongoing innovation powered by in-memory technology. These features help accelerate and streamline business processes. Among its main advantages are enhanced operational efficiency, a simplified data model that allows for quicker processing, and automatic, continuous updates from SAP. These updates not only minimize IT overhead but also ensure access to the latest features, including advancements in AI and machine learning.
dislikes: My main dislikes about SAP S/4HANA Cloud are its high costs and complexity, as well as the steep learning curve it presents. I also find the system to be quite rigid, with limited options for customization and some difficulties related to usability and integration. Additionally, certain older features seem less developed compared to what was available in previous SAP products.
use_case: SAP S/4HANA Cloud addresses challenges such as data silos, slow processing, and manual, error-prone tasks by offering real-time analytics, automation, and a unified, intelligent platform. This leads to benefits like quicker, data-driven decision-making, increased efficiency through automation, cost savings, stronger security, and greater flexibility to adapt to evolving business requirements. Revolutionizes Business Processes with Real-Time

likes: In my experience, this product has been quite straightforward to use once you become familiar with the Fiori layout, and it definitely feels much cleaner compared to older SAP versions. From a user perspective, the implementation process was not too complex, and most of the features I needed for payroll and payment processing were already available. I found myself using it regularly, particularly during payroll cycles and at month-end. The integration with other SAP tools, especially Multi-Bank Connectivity, is seamless, which I consider a significant advantage. Customer support is generally decent, while they aren't always the fastest to respond, they typically resolve issues when you reach out. Overall, the range of features and the strong integration with other SAP applications make it a good fit for daily finance tasks.
dislikes: Some screens can feel a bit too "click heavy," requiring you to go through several extra steps just to accomplish simple tasks. Additionally, with each quarterly update, the UI or certain parts of the process tend to change slightly, which means it takes a bit of time to get used to the new layout. I've also noticed that a few apps can be slow to load during peak times. While none of these issues are major, they can add up and slow you down, especially when you're already busy with payroll or month-end tasks.
use_case: This software primarily eliminates the frustration of dealing with slow and outdated finance processes. With everything updating in real time, I no longer have to wait around for numbers to refresh or for batch jobs to complete during payroll or month end. It conveniently centralizes all payment, approval, and bank related tasks, which genuinely simplifies my workflow. When I used it together with SAP Multi Bank Connectivity, the entire process, from creating payments to receiving bank responses, became much more seamless. Overall, it significantly reduces manual work and allows me to complete tasks more quickly without having to juggle multiple systems. Seamless Integration and User-Friendly Experience for

likes: I greatly enjoy the robustness and flexibility offered by SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The ability to create customizations tailored to the specific needs of the business is something I truly value, as it allows the system to precisely fit our company's requirements. I also like its ability to scale as the company grows, which allows us to expand the service to new areas without making significant changes. The switch to SAP S/4HANA Cloud from SAP FI on premise has been positive due to the advanced capabilities of the SAP HANA database, as well as the cost reductions and improvements in the system's operability and scalability.
dislikes: I find that the interface for the administrative user is not very intuitive or user-friendly, especially for new users, which makes it difficult for them to know how to use the system properly. This requires my team to make a great effort in onboarding new users, providing adequate training or looking for experienced individuals.
use_case: SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides the infrastructure to develop financial and business logic, essential for serving the company and expanding services without major changes, improving operability and scalability, although the interface could be more intuitive for new users. Robustez y Flexibilidad con

Now, implementing and using the system is much easier compared to the past. Furthermore, its compatibility and suitability for end-users have made the adoption process seamless over time, leading to wide acceptance within our organization. This ease of use and functionality has motivated me to recommend it highly, rating it a perfect 10 in likelihood to recommend to others.
dislikes: I feel that AI-driven processes are not yet well integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. likes: I really appreciate the Fiori experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, which offers me an interactive and aesthetically pleasing interface. The AI capabilities integrated into the software are impressive and add significant value to my operations. Furthermore, the appealing look and interactive features of the platform enhance my overall experience. The system’s ease of integration with current compliance forms is another strong suit, making it very straightforward for me to adapt it within existing frameworks. The initial setup process was very simple and fast, which was a pleasant surprise in comparison to other projects I have encountered, allowing for a smooth migration of our entire system. Additionally, the vast array of functionality that SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides suited our needs perfectly, and the software's ability to help us scope and define projects in detail supports our planning and execution significantly. I am also impressed by how we could transition from ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud seamlessly, benefiting from an improved user experience and more encompassing functionality. All these aspects make SAP S/4HANA Cloud an incredible tool that I am more than happy to recommend, rating it a definite ten for recommendation to others.

likes: I appreciate SAP S/4HANA Cloud for its strong integration capabilities and real-time data processing, which are essential for seamless business operations. The transition from traditional ERP systems was smooth, thanks to SAP's structured onboarding support. Its cloud-based model significantly reduces maintenance efforts, eliminating concerns about manual updates, server downtimes, or complex backups, as everything is handled through automated patches and monitoring. I find the built-in analytics and reporting features particularly valuable, as they enable faster, data-driven decision-making. The application supports multiple business functions under one platform, which reduces data fragmentation and improves visibility, enhancing cross-departmental collaboration. The in-memory HANA database is a major advantage, providing real-time performance even with large transactional data volumes. Additionally, I value the automatic updates, cloud security, and minimal maintenance, which allows the team to focus more on optimization rather than system upkeep. The product also offers flexibility and scalability, crucial for growing business needs, and SAP's global support network ensures that it evolves with changing requirements. The Fiori dashboard's user-friendly interface offers a clean, role-based view of reports and tasks, simplifying administration and improving usability. Altogether, SAP S/4HANA Cloud's combination of stability, scalability, and integration provide a complete ecosystem that connects finance, procurement, supply chain, and analytics efficiently, making it one of the most effective ERP platforms we've worked with.
dislikes: I would like SAP S/4HANA Cloud to offer a more customizable user interface and greater system flexibility. While the Fiori interface is user-friendly, some screens feel rigid, making it difficult to tailor workflows or layouts to specific departmental needs without in-depth configuration knowledge. Additionally, the initial configuration and integration setup can be complex for smaller IT teams, so having more guided, automated setup options for data migration or interface mapping would make the adoption process smoother.
use_case: I use SAP S/4HANA Cloud to streamline and centralize business operations, solving data fragmentation and improving visibility. It reduces maintenance efforts, enhances cross-department collaboration, and enables real-time analytics for faster decision-making, optimizing process efficiency and system stability. Reliable ERP with Real-Time Integration, Needs UI

switched_from.reason: We switched from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud to modernize our ERP landscape and take advantage of real-time data processing, better scalability, and integrated cloud capabilities. Compared to the previous system, S/4HANA Cloud is faster, more intuitive with the Fiori interface, and offers real-time analytics instead of batch-based reporting. It has also reduced manual effort through automation and improved integration with other systems like Salesforce and SuccessFactors. Overall, it delivers greater efficiency and transparency across our business processes.

likes: I find SAP HANA Cloud incredibly efficient at handling and processing large volumes of data. Its in-memory computing and seamless integration features allow me to run SQL queries much faster and test ETL pipelines with ease. The real-time dashboards available through SAP HANA Cloud are particularly useful for making quick, informed decisions. The way it integrates easily with SAP and non-SAP applications, as well as APIs and cloud services, enhances its utility for me. I also appreciate the lower cost and scalable infrastructure of the system, which would make me consider purchasing it again. Additionally, setting up SAP HANA Cloud was not difficult, which made the transition smoother for me.
dislikes: I find the system performance during peak data loads problematic. Sometimes, heavy analytical queries or multiple integrations can slow down the response time.
use_case: SAP HANA Cloud helps me process large volumes of data efficiently with in-memory computing and seamless integration, ensuring real-time analytics and quick SQL query execution, simplifying ETL pipeline testing. Efficient Data Management, Needs Better

Moreover, the unified data view across departments enhances collaboration and helps maintain consistency in operations. I have a high appreciation for these aspects, as they collectively enable streamlined and effective project management.
dislikes: I feel that SAP S/4HANA Cloud is expensive to maintain and involves costs for manual scaling, which I find burdensome. Additionally, I believe there is room for improvement in the user interface and the ease of customization, making it more user-friendly and adaptable to specific needs.

likes: What I like best about SAP S/4HANA Cloud is its ability to deliver real-time insights and intelligent automation across core business processes. With its in-memory computing and simplified data model, it significantly enhances performance and allows organizations to make faster, more informed decisions. The cloud-native architecture ensures scalability, flexibility, and lower total cost of ownership, while regular updates keep businesses aligned with the latest innovations and compliance standards. Its seamless integration with other SAP solutions and third-party applications allows for a connected, end-to-end digital ecosystem. The embedded AI, machine learning, and predictive analytics tools further boost efficiency and reduce manual effort. Additionally, the user-friendly Fiori interface improves usability and enhances productivity across roles. For companies looking to stay agile and competitive in a rapidly changing business environment, S/4HANA Cloud offers a future-ready ERP solution that supports innovation, resilience, and sustainable growth. It’s a game-changer for digital transformation.
dislikes: One notable drawback of SAP S/4HANA Cloud is its complexity and steep learning curve, especially for organizations transitioning from legacy systems. The initial implementation can be time-consuming and costly, requiring significant planning, change management, and skilled resources. While the system offers powerful capabilities, configuring it to fit specific business needs often demands external consulting, which can drive up costs. Customization is also more limited compared to on-premise versions, which may restrict flexibility for businesses with highly specialized processes. Frequent updates, although beneficial for innovation, can sometimes disrupt workflows or require additional testing and training. Integration with non-SAP systems may also pose challenges, depending on the architecture. Additionally, licensing and pricing models can be complex and difficult to navigate, making cost estimation and control a concern. Overall, while SAP S/4HANA Cloud is powerful, its complexity, cost, and customization limitations can be significant hurdles for some organizations.

This eliminates inefficiencies caused by fragmented systems and improves collaboration company-wide. The automation of routine tasks through embedded AI and machine learning reduces manual workload and operational errors, allowing teams to focus on strategic priorities. The system also supports compliance with global standards and regulations, minimizing risks and audit issues. For businesses undergoing digital transformation, S/4HANA Cloud provides a scalable, future-ready platform that adapts quickly to change. Personally, the benefit comes from enhanced visibility into operations, improved reporting accuracy, and streamlined workflows. It empowers better planning, quicker responses to market changes, and overall business agility. In short, SAP S/4HANA Cloud solves both operational inefficiencies and strategic limitations, making daily tasks more efficient and outcomes more impactful

likes:
What I like best about SAP S/4HANA Cloud is its ability to deliver **real-time insights and seamless automation** across core business operations. The **in-memory computing** makes data processing incredibly fast, enabling quicker, more informed decisions. I also appreciate the **modern Fiori interface**, which feels intuitive and consistent across devices, making day-to-day navigation smooth. 

The platform’s **AI-driven automation** and **embedded analytics** help improve efficiency, while the **clean core architecture** keeps the system scalable and easy to maintain. The **quarterly innovation releases** ensure we’re always using the latest technologies without disruption. On top of that, its **global compliance support** and **ESG tracking tools** make it future-ready for enterprises operating in dynamic markets.
dislikes: While SAP S/4HANA Cloud is powerful and modern, the **implementation process can be quite complex and resource-intensive**. The **learning curve is steep** for new users, especially when moving from legacy SAP ECC systems. Frequent updates, although beneficial for innovation, sometimes **disrupt custom configurations or workflows** and require additional testing.
use_case:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud solves major business challenges like fragmented data, manual processes, and slow decision-making by bringing all core operations onto a single, unified, cloud-based platform. This has directly benefited the organization through real-time analytics, process automation, and easier scalability.
Key problems it addresses include:
•	Eliminating data silos and messy integration by providing a unified digital core for finance, procurement, and inventory, which reduces errors and improves collaboration across departments.
•	Replacing manual tasks with intelligent automation, making operations faster, reducing processing errors, and freeing up employees to focus on higher-value work. Powerful Real-Time Insights and Automation, but Complex

likes: I’ve been using SAP S/4HANA Cloud for some time now, and overall, it’s been a game changer for our business operations. The real-time insights and integration across departments really help us stay on top of everything—from finance to supply chain—without switching between multiple systems. Being cloud-based, it’s easier to scale and keeps us updated with the latest features without major downtime. The interface is much cleaner and more user-friendly than traditional ERPs, though it took some time to get used to. For companies looking to modernize and streamline their processes, I’d definitely recommend giving it a serious look.
dislikes: What I don’t like about SAP S/4HANA Cloud is that it can be pretty complicated to set up at first. It takes a good amount of time and effort to get everyone trained and comfortable with it. Sometimes the system feels a bit overwhelming, especially if your team isn’t used to SAP products. Also, while the interface is nicer than older versions, it’s not always super intuitive. And because it’s cloud-based, you don’t have as much freedom to customize things exactly how you want compared to on-premise solutions.
use_case: SAP S/4HANA Cloud has really helped us simplify and speed up our invoice management process. Before, handling invoices was time-consuming and prone to errors because data was scattered across different systems. Now, with everything integrated in real-time, we get instant visibility into invoice status, approvals, and payments. Automation reduces manual data entry and errors, which saves a lot of time and avoids delays. This has improved our cash flow management and vendor relationships since payments are more accurate and timely. Overall, it makes the whole invoicing process smoother and much less stressful for the finance team. Love the features of

likes: There are several aspects of SAP S/4 HANA that I appreciate. The user interface is much more intuitive than what I experienced with the older SAP ECC system. I also value the strong integration across finance, procurement, and supply chain modules, which streamlines processes. The real-time analytics and dashboards are particularly helpful for making informed decisions quickly. Additionally, the option for cloud deployment not only reduces IT maintenance costs but also offers greater scalability.
dislikes: There are a few aspects I dislike. The initial implementation is quite complex and demands significant consulting support. Additionally, the licensing costs are high, which can be a challenge for mid-sized companies. Finally, the system's performance is highly dependent on a stable internet connection.
use_case: SAP S/4HANA Cloud has enabled me to streamline procurement and supply chain operations by providing real-time visibility into inventory, suppliers, and spending. likes: SAP is currently the only one on the market that offers high-quality transactional, analytical and logistical data, and the updated SAP Fiori interface is excellent, and you can feel the work the company has done to improve all the program's functions. Integration with other SAP applications makes the software versatile and allows for combinatorial problem-solving, making it relatively easy to manage all business processes from the original interface. The courses offered by the support team are also very reliable, in fact, they have an academy that can certify you and make you an official SAP technician. The costing functions, personal reports and reporting are the highlights of the software, and there is no doubt that a company's organization can be improved with SAP S/4HANA in its portfolio. The intelligence with which the software improves the business process and the speed with which it executes financial issues is an important complement. In our company we currently have more than 15 products, having this program guarantees us a stable economic organization, invoice management, accounts receivable and reliable synchronization.
dislikes: The product has not had an error since its adoption in our organization, and although it is very technical, we would like to improve the interface. The tools are very comprehensive and can better organize all the options so that they are not overloaded, unlike some SAP integration products that can cause difficulties. Our company has always negotiated SAP license prices, but the latest Fiori update has increased operational costs, and I expect prices to improve in the future. We also ran into some errors when doing the calculations. The workshops tend to be very repetitive, and it would be helpful to have more customer support for complaints and bug reports. We need to improve integration with SAP ECC.
use_case: We are a large company with more than 50,000 users. Our specialties are accounting software and services. We use SAP S/4HANA for the management of all the invoices for the software we sell, it is a useful point for the closing of accounts, ledgers, invoice management, and outgoing and incoming invoices. Our experience in national and international banking is accompanied by demanding customers who require optimal calculations, which is why we decided to adopt the platform. It has also worked excellently as an ERP, reports and customized reports allow us to obtain organization and reliability, and our accounts are safe since the adoption of the program. Best

likes:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a range of features and functionality, including financial accounting, supply chain management, project management, and human resources management. It also includes industry-specific functionality for industries such as consumer products, professional services, and retail.

One of the key benefits of SAP S/4HANA Cloud is that it is delivered as a cloud-based service, which means that it can be accessed from anywhere with an internet connection. This makes it easier for businesses to manage their operations and make decisions in real-time.

In addition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud is designed to be easy to use and intuitive, with a modern user interface that is optimized for mobile devices. It also integrates with a range of other SAP and non-SAP applications, allowing businesses to seamlessly connect their various systems and processes.
dislikes:
Like any software solution, SAP S/4HANA Cloud has some potential disadvantages that businesses should consider before implementing it. Here are a few potential disadvantages of SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

Cost: SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a subscription-based service, which means that businesses must pay a monthly or annual fee to use it. This can be a significant cost for some businesses, especially if they have a large number of users or need to use a lot of additional functionality.

Customization: SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a pre-configured software solution, which means that it may not be possible to customize it to meet the specific needs of every business. This can be a drawback for businesses that have very specific or complex processes that require a high level of customization.

Implementation: Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud can be a complex process that requires significant time and resources. It may be necessary to invest in training and consulting services to ensure that the system is implemented and used effectively.

Integration: While SAP S/4HANA Cloud is designed to integrate with other SAP and non-SAP applications, it may still be necessary to invest in additional integration solutions or custom development work to connect it to other systems.

Dependency on the cloud: SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a cloud-based solution, which means that it requires an internet connection to function. This can be a disadvantage for businesses that rely on the system for mission-critical processes and may not have consistent or reliable internet access.

Overall, it's important for businesses to carefully evaluate the potential disadvantages of SAP S/4HANA Cloud in the context of their specific needs and requirements before deciding whether it is the right solution for them.
use_case:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud is designed to help businesses solve a variety of problems related to managing their financials, logistics, and operations. Some specific problems that it is intended to solve include:

Complex and disconnected systems: Many businesses use a variety of different systems and applications to manage different parts of their operations, which can lead to inefficiencies and difficulties in getting a complete picture of their business. SAP S/4HANA Cloud is designed to provide a single, integrated platform that can manage multiple business processes and provide a comprehensive view of a business's operations.

Inefficient processes: Businesses may have processes that are inefficient or require manual effort, which can lead to errors and slowdowns. SAP S/4HANA Cloud includes a range of automation and optimization features that can help businesses streamline their processes and improve efficiency.

Limited visibility: It can be difficult for businesses to get a real-time view of their operations, which can make it hard to make informed decisions. SAP S/4HANA Cloud includes real-time analytics and reporting capabilities that can help businesses gain greater visibility into their operations and make better-informed decisions.

Difficulty adapting to change: Businesses may struggle to quickly adapt to changes in market conditions or customer demand. SAP S/4HANA Cloud is designed to be flexible and scalable, allowing businesses to quickly and easily adjust their operations as needed.

Overall, SAP S/4HANA Cloud is intended to help businesses improve their efficiency, adapt to change, and make better-informed decisions by providing a single, integrated platform for managing their financials, logistics, and operations. Review of

XSA is based on Cloud Foundry architecture and thus supports the notion of “Bring Your Own Language”, allowing developers to develop and deploy applications written in languages and in runtimes other than those XSA implements natively, as well as deploying applications as microservices.

likes: SAP S/4 HANA takes advantages of HANA's multi tenancy feature, with this option we can run ECC and CRM or any other satellite system on one box. A typical ECC - CRM data transfer accounts for 60% of overall system load, with this multi tenancy option this data transfer load will go away.SAP removed the redundant tables required for aggregates and indices. This leads us to fewer table to work upon, that means less table locks, less data to manipulate and better performance
dislikes: SAP HANA is only compatible to and thus will run only on SAP or SUSE Linux certified hardware. reviewer_name: Verified User in Information Technology and Services

likes: SAP is a truly an ERP. It can be used across all departments and across all industries. Can extract real time data to make decisions. Is a great tool from planning to execution to recording data. The security is great. Can give access to users as they are needed. The new G/L feature is amazing. Basically you can keep one books of accounts for both standalone as well as consolidation.
dislikes: The software is extremely expensive and difficult to use. The transition from legacy software to SAP is not easy. You need lot of resources to support the system. Users need to give training to use the software. Sometimes you need to hire outside consultants to solve problems and queries. For some complex problems it can take days to find a solution. Sometimes the users find it complex to use and create mistakes. They don't know how to solve those and needs to go back to the manuals and you lose time in this process.
recommendations: All departments can use the same software. Everything is on one platform. Easy to download the data. All the data flows easily from one department to another. The sales and purchases can be recorded automatically through MIGO and MIRO. Great to handle tons of data. Keeps a track of who does what. Has great audit trail.
use_case: It is a great ERP software. It is very helpful for multinational companies spanning across different continents. The consolidation process is easy if you have subs in multiply countries and follow different accounting styles. The tax process and month end process becomes easy.
reviewer_name: Verified User in Venture Capital & Private Equity

bonus_data.title: An amazing product but difficult to use and implement

dislikes: Could be nicer designed and have more drop down options for users

use_case: S/HANA make sure you have detailed plan laid out before implementation

likes: SAP ERP is an all in one solution for all departments in an organization. HR, Accounts, Finance and Sales - all these departments use SAP for their daily running of the business. SAP can make a business run smooth, efficient and effective. Invoices can be processed, Sales can be tracked, employee work can be measured and financial statements can easily made
dislikes: The interface of SAP ERP is extremely boring to use. Simple colors are used for font and there are no colors. Even a professional may get bored from using this software after a while. This software is also extremely expensive.
use_case: SAP ERP has been extremely helpful in my business. All my accounting problems have been solved by this. Chances of Fraud have been reduced as all my work is being done on cloud. All my purchase orders and invoices can now be made via SAP ERP making my business look more professional. As there are more approvers and checkers involved in this verification process, chances of mistakes are also mitigated.

likes: Forces to somehow move away from VM and use the actual hardware. I like it, even though not everyone does.
dislikes: It seems that SAP miscalculated the UI, again, as I do not consider Fiori particularly well architected
recommendations: This is yet at the stage of try-out as it isn't clear if there's a benefit. For now, it seems SAP imposing this product based on the grounds other than business benefit This isn't anywhere near like R/2 to
use_case:
I think, we need some time to determine if this software takes ground. One thing suspicious about it is that it uses non-widely adopted technologies (e.g. HANA, Fiori) and does not take advantage of the open successful developments (e.g. Hadoop, various Apache projects).
SAP simply may not be able to sustain this development as it requires a pile of resources beyond current SAP capacity (people and money primarily) that aren't fast in the making or cheap, especially that all this requires huge research effort
